axis_descriptions.entity: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.
axis_descriptions.shot: This is the 1st of 1 parts of the video, and it is 600 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 600 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.
axis_descriptions.video: This is the 1st of 1 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=600`.

Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. fields.name: canonical_name
fields.description: Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. These entities are later merged across videos by matching name, so the name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'), which would wrongly merge unrelated people. One name per entity; the description elaborates further.

fields.name: entity_type
fields.description: Whether this entity is a person or a character.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Identifying description of this entity. Elaborate the entity to support its name and future cross-video merging: stable appearance, role, key relationships/interactions, and any grounded aliases or names heard. Prefer visible, spoken, OCR, or credited evidence over guesses.

fields.name: relationships
fields.description: Each relationship starting FROM this entity, output exactly as `dst_name;dst_type;rel_type;desc` (empty array if none). dst_name / dst_type must copy another emitted entity verbatim. rel_type is `actor_portrays_character` (person -> character only; desc must be `other`) or `has_social_relationship` (same type only). desc must be exactly one of family / friend / colleague / acquaintance / rival / other. Do not add evidence text, labels, colons, extra semicolons, or any value outside the enum in this field. Put relationship evidence in the entity description instead. Emit only clearly evidenced ties, not mere co-presence; emit symmetric social ties once.

fields.name: best_thumbnail
fields.description: Best, clearest, most front-facing frame of the entity. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: second_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame as different as possible from best_thumbnail — a different time, ideally a different shot, and a different pose / angle / expression. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: third_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame distinct from and far in time from both other thumbnails, so the three span the entity's varied appearances. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 600 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Describe this shot in rich, verbose detail — cover every meaningful visual and non-visual detail: the actions and interactions, objects, setting, on-screen text, and any spoken dialogue quoted verbatim (with the speaker when certain). Refer to each person or character by the EXACT same name you give it in the `entities` field. Write enough that the shot stands on its own and, read in order, the shots reconstruct the whole story.

fields.name: shot_type
fields.description: The type of the shot.

fields.name: camera_motion
fields.description: The camera motion of the shot.

fields.name: camera_angle
fields.description: The camera angle of the shot.

fields.name: entities
fields.description: Each entity evidenced in this shot, output exactly as `name;entity_type;description`. Include only entities visible, audible, or OCR-referenced in this shot: foreground or speaking persons/characters, important addressed or interacted-with people/characters, and entities needed to understand the action, conflict, portrayal, or relationship; also central places, objects, concepts, or things when they drive the shot's meaning. entity_type is one of person / character / place / object / concept / thing, lowercase. Use exactly two semicolons as separators; do not put semicolons inside name, entity_type, or description (use commas or dashes). For the `name` element (person/character): Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. Decide from all available context, not this shot alone. The name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'). **Use that entity's exact same name in every shot.** For the `description` element: Describe the entity for IDENTIFICATION — so that, from descriptions alone, one can tell which mentions across shots are the SAME person. Write SEVERAL sentences and pack in AS MANY stable identifying properties as you can: any name or alias heard, role, gender, approximate age, ethnicity, hair (color/length/style), facial features, build/height, distinctive marks, and especially their relationship or recurring interaction with other identified entities (e.g. 'the protagonist's mother', 'the man who repeatedly argues with the bald guard'), which strongly anchors identity. Do NOT describe clothing, location, or momentary physical action — they change and do not help identification.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 1st of 1 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=600`.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Title of the whole video; best guess from in-chunk evidence (titles / credits / dialogue).

fields.name: description
fields.description: Summarize this chunk using only evidence from the emitted shot segments — cover essential story beats, key people/characters, important relationships/conflicts, locations, objects, and short grounded dialogue/OCR facts; do not introduce unsupported facts. Integrate events, objects, story context, visual detail, and audio/OCR/transcription facts into one account: names, relationships, intentions, conflicts, interaction evidence, and production cues like 'Action!'/'Cut!' when evidenced. Quote only short exact phrases; do not reproduce the transcript or assert an uncertain speaker.

fields.name: work
fields.description: Optional work title inferred from this chunk (for root candidate materialization).

metadata.description: A woman with dark hair, seen lying injured or dead amidst the rubble of a destroyed bridge. She is a central figure in the tragic backstory shown in the opening flashback.
A young girl with short red hair, wearing a dirty t-shirt. She is seen protecting her younger sister Powder amidst the destruction of the bridge, showing a protective and determined nature.
A bearded man with dark hair, seen walking towards Vi and Powder on the destroyed bridge. He appears to be a father figure or guardian to the sisters.
An older version of Vi, now a young woman with pink hair, wearing a vest and carrying a backpack. She is leading a group of friends on a rooftop heist.

The video opens with a stylized, animated sequence inside a mechanical chamber. A circular disc descends, revealing the 'A NETFLIX SERIES' logo with illustrations of two characters, Vi and Powder, facing each other.

metadata.title: Arcane: League of Legends - Episode 1
metadata.description: The video opens with a dark, red-hued flashback depicting a catastrophic event on a bridge, where a masked enforcer patrols amidst fire and debris. Two young girls, Vi and Powder, are shown grieving over their mother's body before being comforted by a bearded man, Vander. The scene transitions to the title sequence for 'Arcane: League of Legends,' featuring claymation-style figures and credits for Riot Games and Fortiche. The narrative then shifts to a bright, sunny day in the city of Piltover, where Vi, Powder, and their friends Ekko and Claggor are on a rooftop. They discuss their plan to break into an inventor's office, with Ekko expressing his ambition to 'shoot one of them down.' The group navigates the rooftops, with Vi rescuing Powder from a fall. They successfully infiltrate the office, where they marvel at the inventor's creations, including a 'real Hextech' device. The segment ends with the group exploring the room, highlighting the stark contrast between the opulence of Piltover and the struggles of their home.

gemini_result.title: Arcane Opening Sequence
gemini_result.description: The video begins with a record player starting, followed by a flashback to a violent massacre on a bridge shrouded in red smoke. Enforcers with glowing red eyes and gas masks patrol the aftermath. Two young girls, one with pink hair and another named Powder, are seen amidst the destruction, terrified and holding hands. They witness a large man with a beard fighting an enforcer. The man then looks at the girls with pity, drops his weaponized gauntlets, and carries them away from the carnage. The scene transitions to the opening credits of the show 'Arcane'. After the credits, the young girl with pink hair, Powder, and two other boys are seen climbing up to a rooftop, looking out over the pristine city of Piltover.

fields.name: canonical_name

metadata.title: Arcane: The Arcane and the Undercity
metadata.description: This segment opens with a flashback to a young Jinx (Powder) and Vi in a snowy landscape where a mysterious hooded figure uses magic to save Jinx, granting her a blue crystal. The narrative then shifts to the present, where Professor Heimerdinger visits a distraught Jayce in his destroyed laboratory. Heimerdinger warns Jayce about the dangers of the arcane and advises him to admit his mistakes to the Council. The scene transitions to the Undercity, where Vi, Powder, and their friends are preparing for a heist. Powder tests a new weapon, but their hideout is raided by Enforcers led by a masked officer. The segment ends with the group being cornered as the Enforcers search the premises.

A mysterious rune mage appeared, using a glowing blue crystal to cast a protective spell and save them, leaving the crystal with young Jayce. The sequence transitions into the show's stylized opening credits featuring various characters. Afterward, Enforcer Grayson investigates the ruined lab, questioning Jayce about the illegal, restricted items found among the debris.

axis_descriptions.entity: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.

metadata.title: Game of Thrones - The Night Lands
metadata.description: This segment opens with the HBO Entertainment logo and transitions to a scene where three men on horseback, including a central figure in a black fur cloak, ride through a snowy gate holding torches. They travel through a dark tunnel and emerge into a vast, snowy landscape dominated by a massive wall of ice. The group rides into a snowy forest, where one rider dismounts and discovers a circle of dead bodies arranged on the snow. A young girl with pale skin and blue eyes appears, and the rider flees in terror. The group discusses the discovery, with one man urging them to return while another insists on investigating further. The scene culminates in a violent confrontation where the rider is attacked and killed by a figure in a dark cloak. The segment concludes with the show's opening credits, featuring a miniature map of Westeros and the title 'Game of Thrones,' followed by a brief scene of a man being captured by soldiers and a final shot of a young boy practicing archery at Winterfell.

axis_descriptions.entity: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.
axis_descriptions.shot: This is the 1st of 1 parts of the video, and it is 600 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 600 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.
axis_descriptions.video: This is the 1st of 1 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=600`.

fields.name: canonical_name
fields.description: Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. These entities are later merged across videos by matching name, so the name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'), which would wrongly merge unrelated people. One name per entity; the description elaborates further.

fields.name: entity_type
fields.description: Whether this entity is a person or a character.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Identifying description of this entity. Elaborate the entity to support its name and future cross-video merging: stable appearance, role, key relationships/interactions, and any grounded aliases or names heard. Prefer visible, spoken, OCR, or credited evidence over guesses.

fields.name: relationships
fields.description: Each relationship starting FROM this entity, output exactly as `dst_name;dst_type;rel_type;desc` (empty array if none). dst_name / dst_type must copy another emitted entity verbatim. rel_type is `actor_portrays_character` (person -> character only; desc must be `other`) or `has_social_relationship` (same type only). desc must be exactly one of family / friend / colleague / acquaintance / rival / other. Do not add evidence text, labels, colons, extra semicolons, or any value outside the enum in this field. Put relationship evidence in the entity description instead. Emit only clearly evidenced ties, not mere co-presence; emit symmetric social ties once.

fields.name: best_thumbnail
fields.description: Best, clearest, most front-facing frame of the entity. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: second_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame as different as possible from best_thumbnail — a different time, ideally a different shot, and a different pose / angle / expression. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: third_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame distinct from and far in time from both other thumbnails, so the three span the entity's varied appearances. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Describe this shot in rich, verbose detail — cover every meaningful visual and non-visual detail: the actions and interactions, objects, setting, on-screen text, and any spoken dialogue quoted verbatim (with the speaker when certain). Refer to each person or character by the EXACT same name you give it in the `entities` field. Write enough that the shot stands on its own and, read in order, the shots reconstruct the whole story.

fields.name: shot_type
fields.description: The type of the shot.

fields.name: camera_motion
fields.description: The camera motion of the shot.

fields.name: camera_angle
fields.description: The camera angle of the shot.

fields.name: entities
fields.description: Each entity evidenced in this shot, output exactly as `name;entity_type;description`. Include only entities visible, audible, or OCR-referenced in this shot: foreground or speaking persons/characters, important addressed or interacted-with people/characters, and entities needed to understand the action, conflict, portrayal, or relationship; also central places, objects, concepts, or things when they drive the shot's meaning. entity_type is one of person / character / place / object / concept / thing, lowercase. Use exactly two semicolons as separators; do not put semicolons inside name, entity_type, or description (use commas or dashes). For the `name` element (person/character): Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. Decide from all available context, not this shot alone. The name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'). **Use that entity's exact same name in every shot.** For the `description` element: Describe the entity for IDENTIFICATION — so that, from descriptions alone, one can tell which mentions across shots are the SAME person. Write SEVERAL sentences and pack in AS MANY stable identifying properties as you can: any name or alias heard, role, gender, approximate age, ethnicity, hair (color/length/style), facial features, build/height, distinctive marks, and especially their relationship or recurring interaction with other identified entities (e.g. 'the protagonist's mother', 'the man who repeatedly argues with the bald guard'), which strongly anchors identity. Do NOT describe clothing, location, or momentary physical action — they change and do not help identification.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Title of the whole video; best guess from in-chunk evidence (titles / credits / dialogue).

fields.name: description
fields.description: Summarize this chunk using only evidence from the emitted shot segments — cover essential story beats, key people/characters, important relationships/conflicts, locations, objects, and short grounded dialogue/OCR facts; do not introduce unsupported facts. Integrate events, objects, story context, visual detail, and audio/OCR/transcription facts into one account: names, relationships, intentions, conflicts, interaction evidence, and production cues like 'Action!'/'Cut!' when evidenced. Quote only short exact phrases; do not reproduce the transcript or assert an uncertain speaker.

metadata.description: A pregnant woman with long platinum blonde hair, wearing a light purple dress, riding a white horse. She is the central figure in the Dothraki sea scene.
A man with long platinum blonde hair, wearing dark clothing, seen talking to Jorah Mormont in the Dothraki camp.
A short man with curly brown hair, wearing a maroon tunic, seen lying in a stable with dogs and later talking to Joffrey.
A man with long brown hair and a beard, wearing armor and a dark cloak, seen standing with Joffrey and Tyrion.
A woman with long blonde hair, wearing a green floral dress, seen sitting at a dining table with Tyrion and Jaime.
A woman with long brown hair, wearing a dark green dress, seen sitting by a bed with a sleeping child.

The opening sequence of Game of Thrones begins with the HBO logo, followed by a detailed animated map of Westeros and Essos. The camera pans across the map, zooming in on various locations such as King's Landing, Winterfell, and The Wall. Miniature models of castles, trees, and armies grow and move across the map, accompanied by the show's iconic theme music. The sequence ends with the title card 'Game of Thrones' and credits for the writers and director.

metadata.title: Game of Thrones - Cripples, Bastards, and Broken Things
metadata.description: The video opens with the HBO Entertainment logo and the iconic 'Game of Thrones' title sequence, featuring a growing map of Westeros and Essos with miniature castles and trees. The episode begins with Daenerys Targaryen and Jorah Mormont riding through the Dothraki Sea, discussing the Dosh Khaleen and the Dothraki's belief in the end of the world. They arrive at a Dothraki village where Daenerys is greeted by the widows. Viserys Targaryen confronts Jorah, demanding his crown and threatening to stay on the road until he gets it. The scene shifts to Winterfell, where Tyrion Lannister is found sleeping in a kennel with dogs. Joffrey Baratheon and Sandor Clegane find him and order him to apologize to the Starks. Tyrion then joins the Stark family for a meal, where he discusses his upcoming journey to the Wall with Catelyn Stark and Ned Stark. The episode concludes with Catelyn Stark visiting Lyanna Mormont's mother, who is tending to her sick son, and sharing a story about her own lost child.

gemini_result.description: The video begins with the HBO Entertainment logo, followed by the iconic opening credits of Game of Thrones, which feature a mechanical map of Westeros and Essos. After the credits, the scene transitions to a vast grassy plain known as the Dothraki Sea, where a large horde of Dothraki riders is traveling. Daenerys Targaryen, looking exhausted, rides alongside Jorah Mormont, who offers her dried meat and explains Dothraki customs and legends about ghost grass. Viserys Targaryen rides nearby. The horde eventually arrives at a camp, where Daenerys is helped off her horse, barely able to stand. Viserys and Jorah have a conversation about their current situation, Magister Illyrio, and why Ned Stark exiled Jorah. The video ends with a brief shot of dogs resting in a pen.
